ci_support_linux_64_r_base4.4.yaml:
channel_sources:
- conda-forge
channel_targets:
- conda-forge main
cran_mirror:
- https://cran.r-project.org
docker_image:
- quay.io/condaforge/linux-anvil-x86_64:alma9
pin_run_as_build:
  r-base:
    min_pin: x.x
    max_pin: x.x
r_base:
- '4.4'
target_platform:
- linux-64

about.summary: Provides functions to compile and load Rust code from R, similar to how 'Rcpp' or 'cpp11' allow easy interfacing with C++ code. Also provides helper functions to create R packages that use Rust code. Under the hood, the Rust crate 'extendr' is used to do all the heavy lifting.

Provides functions to compile and load Rust code from R, similar to how 'Rcpp' or 'cpp11' allow easy interfacing with C++ code. Also provides helper functions to create R packages that use Rust code. Under the hood, the Rust crate 'extendr' is used to do all the heavy lifting.

name: r-rextendr
raw_meta_yaml:
{% set version = "0.5.0" %}
{% set posix = 'm2-' if win else '' %}

package:
  name: r-rextendr
  version: {{ version|replace("-", "_") }}

source:
  url:
    - {{ cran_mirror }}/src/contrib/rextendr_{{ version }}.tar.gz
    - {{ cran_mirror }}/src/contrib/Archive/rextendr/rextendr_{{ version }}.tar.gz
  sha256: 45f65fc766e2b22886f2e2e8f9fb59b3cda3c7af03878e15c83dfecc303a14ab

build:
  number: 0
  noarch: generic
  rpaths:
    - lib/R/lib/
    - lib/
  missing_dso_whitelist:
    - '*/R.dll'        # [win]
    - '*/Rblas.dll'    # [win]
    - '*/Rlapack.dll'  # [win]

requirements:
  build:
    - {{ posix }}zip               # [win]
    - cross-r-base {{ r_base }}    # [build_platform != target_platform]
  host:
    - r-base
    - r-brio
    - r-callr
    - r-cli
    - r-desc
    - r-dplyr
    - r-glue >=1.7.0
    - r-jsonlite
    - r-pkgbuild >=1.4.0
    - r-processx
    - r-rlang >=1.0.5
    - r-rprojroot
    - r-stringi
    - r-vctrs
    - r-withr
  run:
    - r-base
    - r-brio
    - r-callr
    - r-cli
    - r-desc
    - r-dplyr
    - r-glue >=1.7.0
    - r-jsonlite
    - r-pkgbuild >=1.4.0
    - r-processx
    - r-rlang >=1.0.5
    - r-rprojroot
    - r-stringi
    - r-vctrs
    - r-withr

test:
  commands:
    - $R -e "library('rextendr')"           # [not win]
    - "\"%R%\" -e \"library('rextendr')\""  # [win]

about:
  home: https://extendr.rs/rextendr/, https://github.com/extendr/rextendr
  license: MIT
  summary: Provides functions to compile and load Rust code from R, similar to how 'Rcpp' or 'cpp11' allow easy interfacing with C++ code. Also provides helper functions to create R packages that use Rust code. Under the hood, the Rust crate 'extendr' is used to do all the heavy lifting.
  license_family: MIT
  license_file:
    - '{{ environ["PREFIX"] }}/lib/R/share/licenses/MIT'
    - LICENSE

extra:
  recipe-maintainers:
    - conda-forge/r
    - sgsokol

# Package: rextendr
# Title: Call Rust Code from R using the 'extendr' Crate
# Version: 0.4.2
# Authors@R: c(person(given = "Claus O.", family = "Wilke", role = "aut", email = "wilke@austin.utexas.edu", comment = c(ORCID = "0000-0002-7470-9261")), person(given = "Andy", family = "Thomason", role = "aut", email = "andy@andythomason.com"), person(given = "Mossa M.", family = "Reimert", role = "aut", email = "mossa@sund.ku.dk"), person(given = "Ilia", family = "Kosenkov", role = c("aut", "cre"), email = "ilia.kosenkov@outlook.com", comment = c(ORCID = "0000-0001-5563-7840")), person(given = "Malcolm", family = "Barrett", role = "aut", email = "malcolmbarrett@gmail.com", comment = c(ORCID = "0000-0003-0299-5825")), person(given = "Josiah", family = "Parry", role = "ctb", email = "josiah.parry@gmail.con", comment = c(ORCID = "0000-0001-9910-865X")), person(given = "Kenneth", family = "Vernon", role = "ctb", email = "kenneth.b.vernon@gmail.com", comment = c(ORCID = "0000-0003-0098-5092")), person(given = "Alberson", family = "Miranda", role = "ctb", email = "albersonmiranda@hotmail.com", comment = c(ORCID = "0000-0001-9252-4175")))
# Description: Provides functions to compile and load Rust code from R, similar to how 'Rcpp' or 'cpp11' allow easy interfacing with C++ code. Also provides helper functions to create R packages that use Rust code. Under the hood, the Rust crate 'extendr' is used to do all the heavy lifting.
# License: MIT + file LICENSE
# URL: https://extendr.rs/rextendr/, https://github.com/extendr/rextendr
# BugReports: https://github.com/extendr/rextendr/issues
# Depends: R (>= 4.1)
# Imports: brio, callr, cli, desc, dplyr, glue (>= 1.7.0), jsonlite, pkgbuild (>= 1.4.0), processx, rlang (>= 1.0.5), rprojroot, stringi, vctrs, withr
# Suggests: devtools, rcmdcheck, knitr, lintr, rmarkdown, testthat (>= 3.1.7), usethis
# VignetteBuilder: knitr
# Config/testthat/edition: 3
# Config/testthat/parallel: true
# Encoding: UTF-8
# RoxygenNote: 7.3.2
# SystemRequirements: Rust 'cargo'; the crate 'libR-sys' must compile without error
# NeedsCompilation: no
# Packaged: 2025-08-26 13:21:13 UTC; iliak

Reimert [aut], Ilia Kosenkov [aut, cre] (ORCID: <https://orcid.org/0000-0001-5563-7840>), Malcolm Barrett [aut] (ORCID: <https://orcid.org/0000-0003-0299-5825>), Josiah Parry [ctb] (ORCID: <https://orcid.org/0000-0001-9910-865X>), Kenneth Vernon [ctb] (ORCID: <https://orcid.org/0000-0003-0098-5092>), Alberson Miranda [ctb] (ORCID: <https://orcid.org/0000-0001-9252-4175>)
